chunks.text: For three years I have been practicing the piano every morning.
My effort has slowly been showing progress.
My pursuit of a single piece of music has been long.
Every day, I have continued constant practice.
Sometimes I have struggled, but I have never stopped.
I used to be unable to play fast.
In my previous days my fingers would tangle and the notes would break.
Lately, however, my fingers have been moving in a steady way.
I have felt devoted to the work of making sound.
Practice has gradually been improving, and my ability has been developing.
For several months now, I have seen a slow recent growth in myself.
I played all the way to the last note in a consistent manner.
That was the small achievement that the long continuing time had been quietly leaving for me.
